description_long: This object is a rectangular solar energy infrastructure unit, likely a solar panel array, designed for urban environments. The structure features a flat, dark surface typical of photovoltaic panels, bordered by a robust frame, possibly made of metal. On one side, there are several raised modules or control boxes, which may house electrical components or inverters. The frame appears to be reinforced and slightly elevated, suggesting it is intended for ground or flat-roof mounting. Subtle red accents and a few visible fasteners or connectors are present, indicating points for maintenance or electrical interfacing. The overall design is compact and streamlined, suitable for integration into cityscapes or utility installations.
description: A rectangular, flat solar panel array with a dark surface, metallic frame, and raised control modules on one side. Red accents and visible connectors suggest utility or maintenance features.
description_view_0: Front view shows the narrow edge with raised control modules and red accent on the left.
description_view_1: Side view highlights the flat panel surface, elevated frame, and a visible control box near the center.
description_view_2: Rear view displays the opposite narrow edge, with less visible detail and a more uniform frame.
description_view_3: Top-down view reveals the full rectangular panel surface, control modules, and red accents at both ends.
attribute_mounting_structure_type: ground-mounted, supported by a metallic frame structure
attribute_array_configuration: single row of 1 large panel with attached control modules
